bio: Hwang Youtaek is a South Korean director and creative director.

Working at the intersection of performing arts, technology, and cultural programming, Hwang explores new horizons in performance. Currently leading the interdisciplinary art collective THE STRANGER, the work continues to pursue post-theatrical forms, technological convergence, and projects centred on borders.
In 2018, while undertaking a theatrical wandering project that crossed Eurasia by bus from Korea to the UK, Hwang became interested in expanding what theatre can be.

Since then, the work has continued experimenting with shifting the mediating role between work and audience away from the performer and towards other elements — focusing on post-theatrical approaches in which the audience moves from spectator to becoming part of the work itself.

Since 2021, Hwang has been researching Korea's DMZ and the Mexican border wall, developing work around diverse national boundaries. Beyond this, Hwang also directs performances and festivals that engage with national heritage, exploring the relationship between art, place, and community.
about.studioDescription: Founded in 2016, THE STRANGER is an artist group that creates new artistic languages through the convergence of technology and genre hybridization. Since the 2018 World Wandering Project (9 countries, 18 cities), the group has pursued trans-linguistic artistic experimentation — expanding through a guest lecture at the National School of Arts in Mexico (2019) and the management of a youth artist residency on Jeju Island (2020–2021). Specialists in direction, media, and technology now collaborate to explore international dialogue through the 'Border' series and beyond, continuing the journey as innovative 'strangers' who cross the boundaries of contemporary art and culture.
